shortcuts.name: AI Summarize
shortcuts.summary: Summarize clipboard text with an explicitly declared model action.

shortcuts.name: Clean Clipboard
shortcuts.summary: Normalize clipboard text by trimming whitespace and removing common tracking parameters.

shortcuts.name: Network Note
shortcuts.summary: Fetch a text note from an approved example endpoint and copy it to the clipboard.

hosted_deployment.dry_run_command: uv run shortcutkit html publish path/to/site --provider here-now --dry-run --json
hosted_deployment.publish_command: uv run shortcutkit html publish path/to/site --provider here-now --run --json

input_matrix.notes: Shows a blocking pre-read confirmation, resolves the shared HTML file to renderable text, base64-encodes that render text, and opens Safari.
Extracts the archive, chooses index.html or another HTML file, and rewrites supported same-name asset references.
Scans recursively, chooses index.html or another HTML file, and rewrites supported same-name asset references.

local_bundle.deterministic_command: uv run shortcutkit html bundle path/to/index.html --output path/to/index.bundled.html --json
limitations: Single-file rendering resolves HTML as text and is optimized for normal UTF-8 HTML files, not arbitrary legacy encodings.
Same-name replacement is best-effort and does not understand every nested path form.
Use shortcutkit html bundle for CSS url(), @import, srcset, deterministic hashing, and large-site diagnostics.
data: URL rendering can hit iOS or Safari size limits.

profiles.notes: The shortcut resolves every input type to local render HTML text before opening Safari. Single files are optimized for normal UTF-8 HTML. ZIP and folder inputs also attempt best-effort same-name asset rewriting. Use `shortcutkit html bundle` for deterministic inlining of CSS, JavaScript, images, fonts, manifests, CSS url() dependencies, and srcset into a single self-contained HTML file.

The shortcut always shows the here.now deployment command after the local render attempt. Use `shortcutkit html publish --provider here-now --dry-run` to plan a hosted multi-file Safari runtime; `--run` returns a shareable siteUrl and requires explicit approval.

icon.alt: Open HTML shortcut icon with an HTML document, Safari compass, and share arrow.

shortcuts.name: Open HTML
shortcuts.summary: Try a local bundled Safari render, then offer a here.now share URL path.
